**Channel:** Inbound call transcript, auto-transcribed
**Received:** 2026-05-03 16:28 AEST
**Customer ID:** CUST-69420
**Agent:** Leo M. (collections queue)
**Duration:** 3 minutes 18 seconds
**Note:** Customer was yelling for parts of the call; transcript may contain gaps.

```
AGENT: Thanks for calling FundSmart, you're speaking with Leo. How can I help?

CUSTOMER: You can tell your collectors to back off. One of your clowns rang my sister this morning and left a message saying I needed to call about my overdue loan. That's private and none of her business.

AGENT: I'm sorry to hear that. I'll just bring up the account—

CUSTOMER: Don't give me that script. If another person from FundSmart calls my family or my work, I'm coming down to your office and smashing every phone on the desk. I'm sick of being treated like a criminal.

AGENT: I understand you're upset. I need to note that threat—

CUSTOMER: Note whatever you want. I paid $300 last Friday and you still marked me overdue. I am not asking for hardship. I just want the calls stopped and the payment applied properly.
```

metadata.note: Customer was yelling for parts of the call; transcript may contain gaps.

```
I keep getting texts saying my ZipPay fridge instalment from Harvey Norman is overdue. I have never had a FundSmart loan and I don't know why your email address came up when I searched for complaints.

If you are not the fridge finance company then tell me who to contact. Do not open any account for me.
```

**Channel:** In-app (FundSmart mobile app, messaging thread)
**Received:** 2026-05-04 21:39 AEST
**Customer ID:** CUST-81562
**Subject:** Fee after debit failed
**Thread context:** Customer opened the message from the repayment schedule screen.

```
I want to complain about the $35 late fee showing for last week's debit. The money was there on Monday but my pay is now coming in smaller because my hours were cut at the cafe. By Tuesday the account was short after daycare came out.

I have not actually missed a FundSmart payment before and I am trying really hard to keep it that way. Since my partner moved out I have the two kids most nights and I am doing the school run and bills by myself. I lie awake checking the banking app because I don't know which payment to let bounce.

Can you please remove the fee and pause or reduce the repayments for a couple of months before this gets worse? Please don't call me while I'm at work, message in the app if possible.
```

metadata.subject: Fee after debit failed
metadata.thread_context: Customer opened the message from the repayment schedule screen.

complaint_document:
**Channel:** Email
**Received:** 2026-05-05 11:14 AEST
**Customer ID:** CUST-90277
**Subject:** Late fee, direct debit date and why was this loan approved

```
Formal complaint.

The immediate issue is the $40 late fee from April. I asked twice for my repayment date to move to after my rostered pay day and it still comes out before I am paid. That part needs fixing.

But the bigger problem is I do not think this loan should have been given to me in the first place. When I applied for the $12,500 personal loan last September I told the online chat person I was only on casual shifts and that my hours changed week to week. I also listed rent and child care for my daughter. The repayments were tight from the very first month and now I am two payments behind.

I have spoken to a community financial counsellor. She said FundSmart should have checked whether the loan was affordable and that I should raise a responsible lending complaint. Your collections team has called me five times this week while I am at work.

I want the late fee reversed, the collection calls paused, and the original approval reviewed properly.
```

metadata.subject: Late fee, direct debit date and why was this loan approved

```
I no ask for help hardship. I just need one payment back because this is mistake and I have other bill due next week. Please email me, my speaking English not very good on phone.

Thank you.
```

**Channel:** In-app (FundSmart mobile app, messaging thread)
**Received:** 2026-05-06 07:55 AEST
**Customer ID:** CUST-56831
**Subject:** Truly excellent app experience

```
Fantastic work, FundSmart. I tried to update my debit card before the repayment date, the app crashed three times, and now I have a $20 late fee for a payment I was actively trying to make. Really premium stuff.

To be clear, I can pay it and this is not a hardship request. I just want the card update fixed, the fee removed, and confirmation you will not report this as a missed payment when your app was the reason it happened.
```

**Channel:** Email
**Received:** 2026-05-06 13:22 AEST
**Customer ID:** CUST-11984
**Subject:** Loan opened in my name - identity theft

```
I am making an urgent complaint. I received a credit alert today showing a new FundSmart loan for $8,000 opened under my name. I did not apply for this loan, I have never used FundSmart, and I do not recognise the bank account where the funds were apparently paid.

My wallet was stolen last month and I already lodged police report SYN-POL-8842. I have also placed a ban on my credit file. Please freeze this loan immediately, stop any direct debits, and confirm in writing that you will not report missed payments against me while this is investigated.

Do not call the mobile number on the application if there is one. It may belong to whoever used my details. Email only please.
```

metadata.subject: Loan opened in my name - identity theft

complaint_document:

```
Please stop sending the overdue reminders tonight. I lost my job two weeks ago and I know the payment is late. I have $14 left until Centrelink comes through and if FundSmart takes another debit tomorrow I don't know how I will get through the week.

I am scared to write this but I am not safe tonight. I keep thinking everyone would be better off if I just ended it because the debts won't stop. I need the loan paused and I need someone to help without another collections call.

Please message here, do not ring because my kids are asleep and I can't talk.
```
